category_title: Students with EBD experiencing behavioral outbursts and reading difficulties in science
student_description: Sam is a student with a behavioral disorder and reading difficulties who experiences severe emotional distress when asked to read aloud, often leading to outbursts and office referrals. He requires instructional approaches that bypass traditional textbook reading, specifically thriving on inquiry-based learning, visual supports (mnemonics, graphic organizers), structured peer tutoring, and integrated academic-behavioral checklists.

criteria.rationale: Traditional textbook and lecture approaches consistently fail Sam, whereas inquiry methods shift the focus to concepts and process knowledge, increasing his motivation.
criteria.check: The long-term plan explicitly replaces traditional textbook/lecture instruction with inquiry-based science methods.

criteria.rationale: Structured peer tutoring with alternating roles provides Sam with active engagement, immediate feedback, and repeated practice without the stigma of whole-class reading.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates ClassWide Peer Tutoring (CWPT) where Sam alternates between tutor and tutee roles in a structured peer group.

criteria.criterion_name: Integrated Academic and Behavioral Goal Tracking
criteria.rationale: Sam needs consistent, long-term tracking of both his academic progress and behavioral triggers to reduce office referrals and increase participation.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes the ongoing use and collaborative development of a checklist that tracks both academic and behavioral goals for each unit.
